front: Photograph: shows model Dovima in a black-and-white Dior evening gown (designed by Yves Saint-Laurent) standing between two circus elephants at the Cirque d'Hiver

Work: photobook commissioned by the Amon Carter Museum (Fort Worth); cover features a girl in overalls; includes a hairless man covered in bees (Boyd Fortin, 13-year-old rattlesnake skinner) and a blond boy holding a gutted snake
Artist: fashion and portrait photographer known for minimalist portraits — subjects posed head-on against plain white backgrounds; staff photographer for Vogue and later The New Yorker
Photograph: four solarized psychedelic portraits of the Beatles made in 1967; includes John Lennon with swirling glasses and Ringo with a dove
Photograph: shows actress Nastassja Kinski posing nude with a python coiled around her body
Work: collaboration with childhood friend James Baldwin critiquing American society during the Civil Rights era
back: Nothing Personal (Richard Avedon and James Baldwin)

front: Artist: documented his father Jacob Israel Avedon's final years (1969–1973) in Sarasota, Florida; Janet Malcolm called the series 'anatomies of aging'
Artist: photographed Andy Warhol and his Factory superstars (some nude) in a 1969 series
Artist: provided both inspiration and actual photographs for the 1957 film Funny Face
